name: python
description: Python SDK pipeline adapter. Target: Python 3.12+ async-capable client SDK (motadata-py-sdk or equivalent).

baselines.scope_note: v0.5.0 Phase A scaffold: directory baselines/python/ exists but is empty. Files materialize on first Python pilot run (Phase B). Per Decision D2=Lenient, no contribution to shared-partition partitioning unless Phase B data shows ≥3pp quality_score divergence on a debt-bearer; then that specific agent flips to Progressive (per-language partition) until its Split rewrite ships. See docs/LANGUAGE-AGNOSTIC-DECISIONS.md and docs/R2-DEBT-REWRITE-FEASIBILITY.md.

generalization_debt.resolution_policy: Python pack starts debt-free at v0.5.0 Phase A. Generalization debt is tracked on shared-core (the package whose role is neutral but whose body cites idioms of a specific language) — not on language-adapter packs. If a Python-specific agent or skill is later authored that turns out to also be useful for Rust/TS/Java, it gets moved to shared-core with an entry in shared-core.json's generalization_debt array.

notes.phase_a_scope: Scaffold only. Empty agents/skills/guardrails arrays — Python-specific artifacts authored in Phase B as the first Python TPRD exposes which gaps need filling. Toolchain block declared inline (rule L3); externalization to .claude/package-manifests/python/adapters/*.sh deferred until Phase C touchpoint hardening (T2-7).
notes.shared_inheritance: All shared-core agents (sdk-intake-agent, sdk-design-lead, sdk-impl-lead, sdk-testing-lead, sdk-design-devil, sdk-security-devil, sdk-overengineering-critic, sdk-marker-scanner, sdk-marker-hygiene-devil, baseline-manager, learning-engine, metrics-collector, etc.) and all shared skills (tdd-patterns, idempotent-retry-safety, network-error-classification, etc.) apply to Python runs via depends: shared-core. Per D6=Split, debt-bearer agent bodies stay in shared-core; per-language overrides go in <pack>/conventions.yaml when authored in Phase B.
notes.tier_default: T1 (full perf-confidence regime). Python perf-budget MMD authoring + py-spy/scalene profile parser + asyncio leak harness all materialize lazily during Phase B.
notes.marker_protocol_note: G95-G103 (marker protocol) currently live in go because byte-hash semantics use Go-specific source-byte offsets. When the first Python TPRD lands a [traces-to:] marker, the marker-scanner needs to learn Python's # comment syntax (already declared above in marker_comment_syntax). G95-G103 may need to migrate from go to shared-core, OR get python-specific siblings — decided in Phase B based on observed implementation cost.
notes.pipeline_version_compat_rationale: Python adapter requires v0.5.0+ because that's when this manifest was authored and when the per-language baseline partitioning at baselines/python/ ships.
